R Notebook

library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesianId)

data(mtcars)
data("epilepsy")

https://github.com/paul-buerkner/brms/issues/219 See the second example in the documentation for “stanvar”.

formula = count ~ Trt + zBase

# This is based on the second example in the help file for "stanvar".
intercept_prior = set_prior("", class = "Intercept")
b_prior = set_prior("multi_student_t(1, rep_vector(0.0, Kc), r * sigma^2 * V)", class = "b")
sigma_prior = set_prior("gamma(0.01, 0.01)", class = "sigma") # mimic a Jefferey's prior
our_prior = intercept_prior + b_prior + sigma_prior

stan_code = brms::stancode(object = formula,
                             data = epilepsy,
                             prior = our_prior,
                             stanvars = stanvar(name = "V", scode = "matrix[Kc, Kc] V = inverse(Xc'*Xc);", block = "tdata", position = "end") + stanvar(r, name = "r", scode = "real<lower=0> r;", block = "data")
)

print(stan_code)
// generated with brms 2.23.0
functions {
}
data {
  int<lower=1> N;  // total number of observations
  vector[N] Y;  // response variable
  int<lower=1> K;  // number of population-level effects
  matrix[N, K] X;  // population-level design matrix
  int<lower=1> Kc;  // number of population-level effects after centering
  int prior_only;  // should the likelihood be ignored?
  real<lower=0> r;
}
transformed data {
  matrix[N, Kc] Xc;  // centered version of X without an intercept
  vector[Kc] means_X;  // column means of X before centering
  for (i in 2:K) {
    means_X[i - 1] = mean(X[, i]);
    Xc[, i - 1] = X[, i] - means_X[i - 1];
  }
  matrix[Kc, Kc] V = inverse(Xc'*Xc);
}
parameters {
  vector[Kc] b;  // regression coefficients
  real Intercept;  // temporary intercept for centered predictors
  real<lower=0> sigma;  // dispersion parameter
}
transformed parameters {
  // prior contributions to the log posterior
  real lprior = 0;
  lprior += multi_student_t_lpdf(b | 1, rep_vector(0.0, Kc), r * sigma^2 * V);
  lprior += gamma_lpdf(sigma | 0.01, 0.01);
}
model {
  // likelihood including constants
  if (!prior_only) {
    target += normal_id_glm_lpdf(Y | Xc, Intercept, b, sigma);
  }
  // priors including constants
  target += lprior;
}
generated quantities {
  // actual population-level intercept
  real b_Intercept = Intercept - dot_product(means_X, b);
}
#compiled_model = cmdstanr::cmdstan_model(cmdstanr::write_stan_file(stan_code))
r = 0.5
#family = gaussian()
#family = student()
#family = lognormal()
family = bernoulli()
print(names(family))
 [1] "family"   "link"     "linkfun"  "linkinv"  "dpars"    "type"     "ybounds" 
 [8] "closed"   "ad"       "specials"
print(family$dpars)
[1] "mu"
#family %in% c(gaussian, student, skew_normal, lognormal, gen_extreme_value)

fit = brm(count ~ Trt + zBase, 
         data = epilepsy,
         prior = set_prior("multi_student_t(1, rep_vector(0.0, Kc), r * sigma^2 * V)", class = "b"),
         stanvars = stanvar(name = "V", scode = "matrix[Kc, Kc] V = inverse(Xc'*Xc);", block = "tdata", position = "end") + stanvar(r, name = "r", scode = "real<lower=0> r;", block = "data"))

plot(fit)

fit = brm(mpg ~ cyl + gear, 
                data = mtcars, 
                prior = set_prior("cauchy(0, 0.71*sigma)", class = "b"),
                iter = 11000,
                warmup = 1000,
                chains = 4, # number of chains
                cores = 2,
                refresh = 0, # don't print those annoying iteration updates
                seed = 1212
                )
rmodels = restricted_models(fit$formula)

rmodels$included_table
model_list = rmodels

model_probs = rep(0.0, times = model_list$n_models)
names(model_probs) = model_list$formula_strings
model_probs["count ~ Trt + zBase"] = 0.5
model_probs["count ~ Trt"] = 0.0
model_probs["count ~ zBase"] = 0.2
model_probs["count ~ 1"] = 0.3
print(model_probs)
count ~ Trt + zBase         count ~ Trt       count ~ zBase 
                0.5                 0.0                 0.2 
          count ~ 1 
                0.3 
par_probs = rep(0.0, times = model_list$n_fixed)
names(par_probs) = model_list$fixed_names

for(i in 1:model_list$n_fixed){
  term = model_list$fixed_names[i]
  includes_term = model_list$included_table[names(model_probs), term]
  par_probs[i] = sum(model_probs[includes_term])
}
print(par_probs)

  0.5   0.7
